concepts: daily trend-reset continuation after failed breakdown
shallow pullback resumption with retained higher-timeframe structure

thesis_hint: Explore a new concept this cycle. Recent visible history is still explore-heavy enough to preserve the soft 70/30 doctrine, and the strongest recent concentration is now in 1h exploration batches that keep dying on zero trades. Rotate away from that crowded lane entirely while staying inside the supported universe. Target SOL 1d and demand 3 materially different daily specs that are simpler and more continuous than the recent intraday event grammar: one failed-breakdown continuation branch that only turns on after downside loss is rejected back above a medium anchor, one value-acceptance breakout branch that enters after multi-bar compression resolves and price retains higher value, and one shallow-pullback resumption branch that enters when daily structure holds above a rising anchor without requiring ceremonial confirmation stacking. Do not clone ETH exploit families, do not recycle the dead AXS/VVV/BABY intraday grammar, and do not write ultra-rare daily setups that cannot plausibly clear a low-double-digit trade count over walk-forward.
stop_condition: Stop the batch when either (A) one spec reaches walk-forward QScore >= 1.0 with profit_factor >= 1.12, total_trades >= 10, and max_drawdown_pct <= 12.0, or (B) 4 materially different specs are exhausted without a pass. Abort any spec with total_trades < 8, profit_factor < 1.02, or max_drawdown_pct > 14.0.
rotation_reason: Force exploration this cycle: recent history remains explore-heavy, the last three visible journal entries are all different exploration families, and 1h is the most crowded recent lane, so SOL/1d is the clean supported rotation away from the failing intraday cluster and the ETH-family exploit path.
